horn-mechhelper-exists: A Settlement Keeper is already nearby. Only one can serve a village at a time.
itemdesc-villagerhorn: Can be used to call for help
interact-villager-horn: Aim at the ground nearby and blow it to call for help summoning a Settlement Keeper!
villagerguide-text: Villages and Villagers While exploring the world you may stumble across existing villages — small communities going about their daily lives. Villagers are friendly unless provoked, and some will trade with you on the spot. You can also found your own village. Craft a Villager Horn and blow it to summon a Travelling Trader who sells everything you need to get started. Buy the Mayor's Workstation first — it defines your village center and radius. First Steps Once you have placed your Mayor's Workstation, interact with it to name and establish your village. Choose a good radius — you can update it later. The radius always centers on your Mayor's Workstation. Once your village is established, you can begin placing the other essential structures. Shelter Every villager needs a bed inside a building to sleep in at night. A bed placed outdoors without walls and a roof cannot be claimed. Villagers also like to gather at a fire brazier in the evening to socialize. Hiring Villagers Villagers have basic requirements before they can be hired. When hiring a new villager all requirements must be met, and there must be a free indoors bed available. If requirements are not met you will be notified of what is missing. Farmer Farmers till soil, plant seeds, water crops, and harvest throughout the day. Requirements: • Workstation must be within 20 blocks of farmland • Village needs 20 farmland blocks per farmer (40 for two, 60 for three, and so on) Shepherd Shepherds tend livestock — wandering among animals, filling troughs, and helping domesticate animals caught from the wild. Domestication takes a generation 0 animal and makes them generation 1, though this is a difficult process. The Shepherd will announce when they are successful. Requirements: • Workstation must be near your barns, animal pens, and troughs • One trough per shepherd in the village minimum • 5 enclosed animals per shepherd (10 for two, 15 for three, and so on) • Only gendered livestock count (sheep, pigs, goats, etc.) — snails and other non-livestock creatures do not • Animals must be inside a pen (fence or gate blocks only, max 15×15) or inside a barn (a fully enclosed room with no exits and minimal skylight) Smith Smiths work the forge during the day, hammering metal for the village. They require a properly equipped Smithy. Requirements: • Workstation must be inside a building • The Smithy must contain an anvil, a forge, and a light source (lamp or torch) • No other professions' workstations in the same room • Maximum 2 smiths per room — build a second smithy if you need more Herbalist Herbalists gather and process plants for the village's medicinal needs. They work out of an Apothecary. Once hired, they will plant horsetail in a flower pot themselves. Requirements: • Workstation must be inside a building • The Apothecary must contain a table, barrel, reed basket or chest, cabinet, flower pot, and a light source • No other workstations can be in the Apothecary Trader Traders run a shop inside your village, offering goods to visitors. Requirements: • Workstation must be placed inside a building • The Shop must contain a table, crate, chest, shelf, and a light source • No other workstations can be in the shop Soldier / Archer Soldiers and Archers defend the village against hostile creatures. They train and operate from the Village Barracks. During temporal storms, non-combat villagers will flee to the barracks for shelter. Soldiers patrol and fight in melee; Archers engage from range. Requirements: • Workstation must be inside a building with at least a 7×7 block floor area • The Barracks must contain a tool rack loaded with a spear (soldiers) or bow (archers) • No civilian workstations in the Barracks Baker Bakers keep the village fed, working at the clay oven throughout the day. Requirements: • Workstation must be inside a building • The Bakery must contain a clay oven, firepit, barrel, storage vessel, and a table — the baker will fill the barrel with water themselves • No workstations other than other Bakers can be in the room Travelling Traders and the Market Stall You can purchase a Market Stall from the Village Trader. Place it in a clear open area and your village will begin receiving visits from travelling traders — the same type you may have encountered at outposts in the world. The trader will announce their arrival in the morning and leave in the evening. Villager Reassignment Villager workstations and beds can be reassigned by right-clicking them. Workstations may only be reassigned by matching profession, but beds can be assigned to any villager you choose.
management-destroy-village-text: Are you sure to permanently destroy this village?
management-no-village-found: This block is currently not connected to a village. Do you want to found a new one?
management-emtpy: The village seems to be empty.
management-poi-stats: This village has a total of {0} beds, {1} workstations and {2} gatherplaces. It's the residence of {3} villagers. The villagers and workstations are split up the following way:
management-market-stall-placed: Market Stall: Placed — travelling traders will visit this village.
management-market-stall-missing: Market Stall: Not placed — buy one from your resident trader to attract travelling merchants.
assign-no-compatible: No compatible villagers in this village.
trader-arriving: A travelling trader named {0} is visiting {1} today. Come see them at the market stall!
trader-departing: The travelling trader {0} is packing up and leaving {1}. Safe travels!
quest-gatherMeatForSeed-desc: My diet could use a little bit more variation. If you provide 25 meat of any kind, I could offer you some seeds in return.
quest-gatherMeatForGrain-desc: My diet could use a little bit more variation. If you provide 20 meat of any kind, I could offer you some grain in return.
quest-gather10MeatFor4FlaxSeeds-title: There is no protein in flax fibre
quest-gather10MeatFor4FlaxSeeds-desc: My diet could use a little bit more variation. If you provide 10 meat of any kind, I could offer you some seeds in return.
quest-gather10MeatFor4ParsnipSeeds-title: There is no protein in parsnips
quest-gather10MeatFor4ParsnipSeeds-desc: My diet could use a little bit more variation. If you provide 10 meat of any kind, I could offer you some seeds in return.
quest-gather10MeatFor4TurnipSeeds-title: There is no protein in turnips
quest-gather10MeatFor4TurnipSeeds-desc: My diet could use a little bit more variation. If you provide 10 meat of any kind, I could offer you some in return.
quest-gather10MeatFor4CarrotSeeds-title: There is no protein in carrots
quest-gather10MeatFor4CarrotSeeds-desc: My diet could use a little bit more variation. If you provide 10 meat of any kind, I could offer you some seeds in return.
quest-gather10MeatFor4OnionSeeds-title: There is no protein in onions
quest-gather10MeatFor4OnionSeeds-desc: My diet could use a little bit more variation. If you provide 10 meat of any kind, I could offer you some seeds in return.
quest-gather10MeatFor4SpeltSeeds-title: There is no protein in grain
quest-gather10MeatFor4SpeltSeeds-desc: My diet could use a little bit more variation. If you provide 10 meat of any kind, I could offer you some seeds in return.
quest-cull2HaresFor20Carrots-desc: Those horrible hares keep eating my crops. Get rid of them and I'll share some carrots with you.
quest-cull2HaresFor20Spelt-desc: Those horrible hares keep eating my crops. Get rid of them and I'll share some spelt with you.
quest-sell1CheesewheelFor4Gears-title: Would love cheese for my bread
quest-sell1CheesewheelFor4Gears-desc: I made some gears from a passing traveler. I would be happy to buy some cheese from you if you have a wheel spare.
quest-gather12CharcoalFor4Gears-desc: I want to produce some new iron tools but forgot to prepare the charcoal. Can you help me gather some coal for me? I could pay you.
quest-buy1BlackGuardSword-title: Creating a weapon of great power
quest-buy1BlackGuardSword-desc: If you hand me the materials as well as some payment for my work, I could create a weapon of exceptional quality for you.
quest-buy1BlackGuardSword-obj:
Gathered iron ingots: {0}/1
Gathered charcoal: {1}/1
Gathered rusty gears: {2}/4
quest-upgradeGambison-desc: With payment and a set of gambison I could tailor it to fit you better I reckon.
quest-upgradeGambison-obj:
Gambison head: {0}/1
Gambison body: {1}/1
Gambison legs: {2}/1
Gathered rusty gears: {3}/32
quest-gather16HayFor1BlueCheesewheel-des: I need help gathering Hay for the lifestock, gather 16 of it and I can share some cheese from my stockpile.
quest-gather16HayFor1CheddarCheesewheel-: I need help gathering Hay for the lifestock, gather 16 of it and I can share some cheese from my stockpile.
quest-gather6GrainFor12Eggs-desc: My hens need more grain to keep up how many eggs they are laying. I will happliy give you a dozen eggs for some grain.
quest-gather24GrainFor18Poultry-desc: My chickens have grown old and fat. I need to make room for the younger generations to grow properly. For some simple grain I can offer you some meat.
quest-gather24GrainFor18Redmeat-desc: My sheep have grown old and fat. I need to make room for the younger generations to grow properly. For some simple grain I can offer you some meat.
quest-kill2WolvesFor8Gears-desc: The wolves are loosing their respect for fire. They are getting closer and closer to the cattle I am herding. I can compensate you if you fend them off.
quest-kill3FoxesFor8Gear-desc: Recently a fox broke into my chicken coop and decimated my lifestock. I do not want this to happen again. If anyone is able to hunt down six of these foxes I would be willing to pay a gear for it.
quest-kill50DriftersFor20Gears-desc: The woods simply aren't safe anymore. Drifters are everywhere. If you could clear the surrounding area from drifters, we could pay you a fair price.
quest-kill2NightmareDriftersFor12Gears-t: The growling from the unspeakable depths
quest-kill2NightmareDriftersFor12Gears-d: We can no longer extract minerals from the mountains because of the creatures that lurk in the caves deep down below. We would be willing to pay a generous price for anyone helping to clear the depths from nightmare drifters.
quest-recruitvillager-desc: For a small amount of gears I can escort and protect you for a day.
quest-repairingAnOldSet1-desc: I need a copper ingot for a new medical needle. I have an old set of armor that I can give you piece by piece if you keep working with me.
quest-repairingAnOldSet2-desc: I need some flaxtwine to make bandages and to fix the hole in this armor set I'm giving to you.
quest-repairingAnOldSet3-desc: If you can get me food for winter I will give you the last piece of the armor set I have.
quest-sell24HorsetailFor3Gears-desc: Supplys are running low and I need some more horsetail, I can pay of cause.
quest-sell10PowderedSulfurFor2Gears-desc: Supplys are running low and I need some more powdered sulfur, I can pay of cause.
quest-healplayersetup-desc: To be able to act as your healer I require some basic things. Stuff for bandages, a stone and stick for crushing herbs and honeycomb to treat wounds. I can become self sufficient after you get me these starting things.
quest-healplayersetup-obj:
Gathered horsetail: {0}/12
Gathered stone: {1}/1
Gathered stick: {2}/1
Gathered honeycomb: {3}/2
Gathered cattails: {0}/12
quest-healplayer-desc: If you ever take a beating, I am able to take a look at your wounds.
dialogue-greet: Oh, nice to meet you {playername}. I am new here. Is there something I can do for you?
dialogue-welcomeback: Welcome back {playername}! Nice to see you.
dialogue-openquests: Got anything you need help with, {npcname}?
dialogue-no-quests: I am fine, but thanks for offering help in these daring times.
dialogue-mechgreet: I am now active and ready to serve. I am {npcname}, an Mechanical Entity created by bzzzt-error-bzzt to aid in Village Management and success of project Regenesis. Identity of {playername} confirmed in bzzt-error-bzzt archives. Your authority as Administrator of this Village is recognized.
dialogue-mechwelcomeback: Administrator {playername}... you have returned. How may I be of service?
dialogue-mechopentrade: (trade) I need Items for the Village, {npcname}.
dialogue-mechopenquests: Got anything you need help with, {npcname}?
dialogue-mechno-trading: My apologies - My stocks are exhausted.
dialogue-mechno-quests: I exist to serve you and the mission of surface reclaimation. I have no other tasks at this time.
dialogue-mechregenesis: Project Regenesis was established by Jon-bzzt-error-bzzt-alx as part of The Great Plan - or rather following this operation. It is what comes after: Reclaimation of the surface world then reestablishment of Villages which will eventually grow into towns... into cities... into nations and great populations. They will provide community and safety for humans to live in the sun once more as they should...
dialogue-mechregenesis2: The Regenesis employs the use of a Bespoke Translocator Network, known as the Regenesis Archive, to bring Stasis-held Humans, volunteers for the Project, to take up their former professions in the Recovering World. I am a unit designed to assist administrators such as yourself with these goals by constructing Workstations and other provisions for the establishment of stable villages....
dialogue-mechregenesis3: I require Gears in order to power the Translocation engine in which to provide these resources for you. I can also convert Parts and other things into gears (trade menu). The Translocation of Villagers will also consume gears when used via the Mayor Workstation.
dialogue-mechvillagefounding: In oder to begin the establishment of the Village under your administration as 'mayor' you will first need to acquire a Mayor Workstation from me. This will allow you to set the parameters of your Village and access the Translocation archive. After this you should plan on your next steps. A Farmer or Shepherd will be required to provide food for any furture Villagers, but they have thier own requirements which must be met.
dialogue-mechadmin: I am here to serve, which topic requires clarification
dialogue-mechadminjobs: Villagers were selected based on their professions in the Time Before the Rot. The Regenesis Archive has villagers of the following professions: Farmers. Shepherds. Herbalists. Traders. Blacksmiths. Bakers. Soldiers and Archers. bzzt-error... 7 additional enteries corrupted...archive recovery in progress... duration unknown. Do you wish to know more?
dialogue-mechadminjobsfarmershep: Farmers and Shepherds provide sustainable food for your villagers. Farmers require a workstation to be placed near at least 20 units of farmland in order to be viable to sustain further villagers. Shepherds tend to animals and can help domesticate them. Shepherds require that you place their workstations near where you keep your animals and that the animals have troughs for feed.
dialogue-mechadminjobstrader: The Trader will buy and sell goods between other villagers and with travelling villagers as well as some stocks they will bring with them from the Regenesis Archive. You will need to ensure the construction of an appropriate shop for them to carry on with their business. A table... plenty of light... a shelf... crate... and chest will help them get a foothold and begin the reformation of an economy.
dialogue-mechadminjobsherbalist: An Herbalist is one specialized in maintaining the health of the citizens of your village. They will need an appropriate Apothecary Shop in order to carry on with their function. A table and barrel and a cabinet A flowerpot and chest made of reeds along with a good source of light will form the basis of an appropriate Apothecary. Once they have established themselves they will provide bandages and healing for you and the villagers.
dialogue-mechadminjobssmith: A Blacksmith - or Smith - is crucial to the progression of things within the Project and your village as they work the various metals smelt from ores. They will require a Smity in which to work. A proper smithy needs an Anvil and a Forge.
dialogue-mechadminjobssoldier: Security is paramount in these times as unknown threats - both of nature or man and perhaps even the supernatural might persist even upon this Tabula Rasa of a world. Soldiers and Archers - loyal fighters all - are awaiting your call to provide protection. Soldiers shall patrol the night, keeping the village safe in the dark. Archers will patrol the day, as danger has no schedule. In order to recruit these valiant warriors to your village you will need to establish a proper Barracks. This room can be no smaller than 7 units by 7 units and must be supplied with Spears (for soldiers) and Bows (for archers) stored upon racks within. They will train for combat here. Also, the Barracks will server as a secure location during emergencies such a harsh weather or brutal storms. Villagers have been trained to seek out the Barracks for defense.
dialogue-mechadminjobsbaker: Bakers work from their Bakeries process the grain farmed by our Farmers into bread which further expands the food stocks of our Village. A Bakery must of course have an Oven... a table... a firepit... a barrel... and a Storage Vessel in order to fit the demands of the role - the baker will fill the barrel with water themselves.
dialogue-mechadminjobsother: Oh yes I apologize for the erorrs. Something seems to have partially corrupted the Archive. It is not a worry. that information is being recovered. Logic suggests that more Villagers of other professions are stored within this portion of the Archive. You should be able to hire them as this information is recovered.
dialogue-mechadmingathering: The Brazier servers as a designator for an area of the Village in which your Villagers can engage in recreational activities. Socializing is the main activity which they will partake in... given their assumedly lonely state in the Underground. Villagers will gather here in the evening. You should acquire one as soon as possible to keep Villager Morale high, and possibly encourage the initial steps towards procre-bzzt-error. Repopulation metrics missing.
dialogue-mechadminbeds: These Beds are not just any beds, but are made of a special material which both serves the comfort of the Villagers post-Archive-Stasis, and provides a Resonance Point for Optimial Translocation Materialization via the gear consumed in order to make it functional (the gear spent to buy it). Translocation is a tricky process and your Villagers need time to stabalize in this arrangement of matter. For the security of your Villagers, you will need to provide them a proper space for their beds. A room indoors will suffice, but having their own space, and especially their own houses, will improve mood and lift spirits. One step at a time Administrator.
dialogue-mechadminwaypoints: Waypoints are charged with a small amount of energy from the gear provided. This energy causes oscilations through the ether which can be felt subconciously by the Villagers as it is closely tied to the energies of the Regenesis Archive. This subconcious signal will help guide the Villagers around the village as it grows larger. Think of these as enhanced street signs!
dialogue-mechadminmarketstall: The Marketstall is very useful in order to send a subtle signal to the various travelling traders which wander the lands between villages. These traders, attuned to the resonance energies within the gears used to create these stations, will seek out your village and trade with you. I would not expect them to linger more than a day, as they are always seeking further opportunities. If I detect a 'handshake' signal from one of these Travellers... I will inform you.
dialogue-mechadminremove: If you need to... you may send a Villager back into stasis within the Regenesis Archive where they will safely await hiring by yourself or another Village's Administrator.
dialogue-mechadmindestroy: It is your right as a bzzt-erorrrrrreda-Ser-bzzt to declare an area unfit for the Village and Destroy its entry within the Archive. This will not remove any Village Structures... but has the risk of Disconnecting any Villagers who have not been sent back to stasis first. Your judgement on this matter will always be respected. You may need to call upon another Settlement Keeper if you require further fabrication. My energies are limited as they are.
dialogue-mechbeforetimes: This unit was not established to interface with other Archives which would hold the information you seek. I am set by virtue of the clockwork which runs me to look ahead... not behind. If I do have such information it is limited.
vsvillage:soldier-spotted-enemy: A soldier of {0} reports contact with {1}!
vsvillage:archer-spotted-enemy: An archer of {0} reports contact with {1}!
